headline: Keeper Security surpasses $225M in ARR with transformative growth and is emerging as the market standard for AI-native identity security
summary: Keeper Security ("Keeper" or "we"), the identity security platform for humans, machines and AI agents, today announced a major milestone in its journey to become the market standard for AI-native identity security, having reached $225 million in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR). Since 2021, Keeper's ARR has grown over 3x.

headline: This Earnings Season Is the Stock Market’s Biggest Test
summary: Baseball legend Tommy Lasorda, the former Los Angeles Dodgers manager who won two World Series, didn’t have much time for the idea of “pressure” in professional sports.  Stock markets are different matter, however.  The looming second-quarter earnings season—set against the pullback in big tech stocks, the resurgence of global geopolitical risks, and the specter of renewed inflation—has investors on edge.

Stock markets are different matter, however.  The looming second-quarter earnings season—set against the pullback in big tech stocks, the resurgence of global geopolitical risks, and the specter of renewed inflation—has investors on edge.

headline: Google appeals Indian ruling over its ads platform, citing consumer harm
summary: Google has challenged an Indian court ruling that it infringed on a company's trademark rights by allowing rivals to use its name as an advertising ‌keyword, arguing the decision will hurt consumers, documents reviewed by Reuters show.  The May decision could reshape the ‌online ads market in a country where Google last year earned $4.1 billion in gross advertising revenue but where it is also facing a ​raft of antitrust cases and court battles.  To ensure their ads are promoted by Google and target the right customers, companies bid on keywords that online consumers type into the search engine.

content.title: Google appeals Indian ruling over its ads platform, citing consumer harm
content.summary: Google has challenged an Indian court ruling that it infringed on a company's trademark rights by allowing rivals to use its name as an advertising ‌keyword, arguing the decision will hurt consumers, documents reviewed by Reuters show.  The May decision could reshape the ‌online ads market in a country where Google last year earned $4.1 billion in gross advertising revenue but where it is also facing a ​raft of antitrust cases and court battles.  To ensure their ads are promoted by Google and target the right customers, companies bid on keywords that online consumers type into the search engine.

thumbnail.caption: FILE PHOTO: The Google logo is seen outside the company's office in London

headline: Bond issuance backing AI investment tops $250B, testing limits of voracious investor demand

headline: Shifting Fed Policy and Rising Bond Yields Could Finally Crack the Stock Market
summary: The Federal Reserve’s first policy meeting under Chairman Kevin Warsh highlighted a big focus on artificial intelligence spending.  Bond markets are listening.  Minutes of the Fed’s June meeting, where interest rates were kept unchanged at between 3.5% and 3.75% but communication was sharply pared back under Warsh’s new leadership, suggested a hawkish stance among Fed governors, many of whom remained focused on inflation pressures in the world’s biggest economy.

content.title: Shifting Fed Policy and Rising Bond Yields Could Finally Crack the Stock Market
content.summary: The Federal Reserve’s first policy meeting under Chairman Kevin Warsh highlighted a big focus on artificial intelligence spending.  Bond markets are listening.  Minutes of the Fed’s June meeting, where interest rates were kept unchanged at between 3.5% and 3.75% but communication was sharply pared back under Warsh’s new leadership, suggested a hawkish stance among Fed governors, many of whom remained focused on inflation pressures in the world’s biggest economy.

headline: AI Infra Summit: the world's only full-stack AI Infrastructure conference shares expanded speaker list
summary: AI Infra Summit, the world's only full-stack AI Infrastructure conference, has expanded its roster of speakers to over 400. The conference will feature senior speakers from world-leading AI pioneers, as they set out their AI Roadmaps for 2027 and beyond. These now include Google, Meta, Amazon, Intel, NVIDIA, US Bank, Oracle, HPE, Liquid AI, Digital Realty, Agility Robotics and Philips.

headline: Broad Market Gains Offset Big Tech Weakness
summary: The S&P 500 was up 0.3%, while the Nasdaq was up 0.2%, and the Dow gained 0.2%.  The S&P 500 Equal Weight Index was up 0.6%, with 316 of the S&P 500’s components in the green.  The broad rally helped offset weakness in the largest names by market capitalization.

content.title: Broad Market Gains Offset Big Tech Weakness
content.summary: The S&P 500 was up 0.3%, while the Nasdaq was up 0.2%, and the Dow gained 0.2%.  The S&P 500 Equal Weight Index was up 0.6%, with 316 of the S&P 500’s components in the green.  The broad rally helped offset weakness in the largest names by market capitalization.

headline: Alphabet (GOOGL) Backs Proxima Fusion In €411 Million Funding Round With RWE
summary: Alphabet's Google and German utility RWE have invested in Proxima Fusion, a company focused on nuclear fusion technology. The funding round totals €411 million, with Alphabet participating as a strategic investor alongside energy sector partners. The move extends Alphabet's reach into long term, carbon free energy solutions beyond its core technology operations. Alphabet, traded as NasdaqGS:GOOGL, is stepping further into energy infrastructure with this Proxima Fusion investment, adding a...

headline: Nasdaq Leads the Market's Climb Higher
summary: All three major indexes are marching higher, following the Nasdaq’s lead. The tech heavy Nasdaq is up nearly 1.2%, followed by the S&P 500, up 0.8%. The Dow is up 196 points, or 0.4%. Broad participation is supporting the move higher, with the majority of Nasdaq and the S&P 500 components in the green.

headline: SK Hynix's US listing, Delta earnings, housing bill: What to Watch
summary: Asking for a Trend Host Josh Lipton previews several of the biggest stories to come tomorrow, Friday, July 10, including SK Hynix (000660.KS) making its debut on the Nasdaq, Delta Air Lines (DAL) earnings, monthly sales results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSM), and the pending approval of Congress' housing affordability bill.
